title: China launches missile from submarine, sparking international concern
summary: Several countries express concern over the launch of a long-range missile from a Chinese submarine amid analyses of its nuclear capabilities.

title: Spain beats Portugal and advances to World Cup quarterfinals without conceding a goal
summary: The Spanish team defeated Portugal 1-0 with a goal in added time, keeping a clean sheet, while Cristiano Ronaldo confirmed that this was his last World Cup.

title: Murderer of student in Asahikawa sentenced to 27 years in prison
summary: The court handed down a 27-year sentence to the 23-year-old defendant for the murder of a high school student in Hokkaido, with neither party appealing.

title: FIFA gives conditional suspension to US striker after Trump's request
summary: The world football governing body delayed the sanction for the US striker for a year after President Trump requested a review of the case.

title: Hamas dissolves Gaza civil administration in gesture toward peace
summary: The Palestinian group announced the dissolution of the Strip's administrative body, though it did not mention disarmament, in an attempt to advance the peace plan.

title: Japanese household spending falls 0.4% in May, sixth consecutive month of decline
summary: Concern over rising prices led consumers to cut spending on travel and other goods, according to the household survey.

title: Real wages in Japan rise 1.4% in May for fifth consecutive month
summary: Wage growth remains positive thanks to moderating inflation and wage increases, according to the Ministry of Labor.

title: Woman dies in hit-and-run in Osaka
summary: An elderly woman died after being struck by a vehicle on a municipal road in Sennan, Osaka; police are searching for the driver.

title: Shizuoka governor approves start of construction for maglev train line
summary: After years of delays, the construction of the Shizuoka section of the magnetic levitation bullet train gets the green light, though technical and land acquisition challenges remain.

title: Belgium thrashes US 4-0, advances to World Cup quarterfinals
summary: The Belgian team decisively beat the US in the round of 16 match, securing their place in the next round.

title: First Pokémon-themed airport opens in Noto region
summary: The Noto airport, damaged by the earthquake, is reborn with Pokémon decorations to boost tourism recovery in the area.

title: Toyota to move part of Tacoma pickup production from Mexico to Texas
summary: The Japanese automaker will invest $5.8 billion in a Texas plant, creating more than 2,000 jobs, to produce the Tacoma model.

title: Dow Jones closes above 53,000 points for the first time
summary: The New York stock index reached a new all-time high, driven by confidence in the US economy and earnings season.

title: Russia launches major attack on Kiev, leaving at least 25 dead
summary: On the eve of the NATO summit, Russian forces bombarded the Ukrainian capital in one of the deadliest attacks in recent weeks.

title: Mass funeral in Tehran for Iran's Supreme Leader Khamenei
summary: Thousands of people accompanied the funeral procession of Ayatollah Khamenei, while the succession of his son Mojtaba is anticipated.

title: Japanese parliament debates reform of Imperial House law without consensus
summary: The government and opposition remain at odds over amending the imperial succession law, with the Lower House blocking progress on the reform.

title: Samsung's operating profits surge 19-fold in second quarter
summary: The South Korean tech giant reported a record profit of 9 trillion won thanks to booming demand for AI chips.

title: Microsoft to cut 4,800 jobs in Xbox and other divisions
summary: The tech company is redirecting resources toward artificial intelligence and reducing staff in its underperforming business units.

title: Typhoon No. 9 approaches Okinawa with great intensity
summary: The typhoon, with very strong winds, is expected to approach the Okinawa region from August 10, so the population is urged to take precautions.

title: JAXA strengthens preventive measures after H3 rocket launch failure
summary: The Japanese space agency will implement stricter expert reviews to prevent future failures in the H3 rocket launch.
